text: You are not here to prove anything to anyone—only to understand a little more.
What you record today will not become a score, only a part of you.
No one is watching you. That is exactly the point.
If you stop, no one will remind you—but you can always start again.
Learning is not continuous. It allows interruption, and it allows return.
What you are doing now may only make sense months later.
Progress is not always visible, but it always leaves a trace.
There is no evaluation system here—only you and your time.
You are allowed to go slower. Nothing here reduces your potential.
Some understanding only happens after it appears three times.
You do not need to finish. You only need to continue.
If you learned nothing today, nothing will be removed.
You are not chasing progress—you are building patience.
Many changes happen when nothing seems to be changing.
This place does not reward speed. It records truth.
You are allowed to stay at one small point for a long time.
Learning is not linear. It behaves more like an echo.
You do not need to improve every day. You only need not to give up completely.
It is normal not to understand things yet.
The fact that you are here already means something has begun.
